changes.before: The kitchen told the story quickly.
changes.after: The half-eaten loaf, the shoved-back chair, and the untouched dust fixed the timing at a glance.

changes.before: The village had been emptied from the inside out, leaving only the shape of what had lived there.
changes.after: The houses still held meals, tools, and beds; only the people were gone.

changes.after: The houses still held meals, tools, and beds; only the people were gone.

changes.before: What remained was faster, harder-edged, and Jaina gave it to Sylvanas at full speed.
changes.after: Jaina's answer came fast and clipped, stripped of the careful border-post phrasing.

changes.before: "The granary shouts loudest, but that doesn't make it the whole answer."
changes.after: "The granary reads strongest. Grain stored here may be the thickest patch. It may be one of a dozen."

changes.before: "The granary shouts loudest, but that doesn't make it the whole answer."
changes.after: "The granary reads strongest. Grain stored here may be the thickest patch. It may be one of a dozen."

changes.before: That was enough.
The account came in pieces.
changes.after: Miri answered in scraps.

changes.before: The lane went still.
changes.after: No one in the lane moved.

changes.before: The lane went still.
changes.after: No one in the lane moved.

changes.before: That and the churned ground said the same thing.
changes.after: Miri's account and the churned ground pointed the same way.

changes.before: Jaina had not made the reading for her. She had only cut the noise away.
changes.after: Jaina had cut the noise away for her.

changes.before: It was calculation.
Sylvanas watched them go until the bend took them.
changes.after: They rode north until the bend took them.

changes.before: The pause had the slower drag of sorting impressions that once seemed unimportant.
changes.after: He took a moment over that, picking through what he'd seen on the road before he answered.

changes.before: He shrugged.
changes.after: One hand lifted off the reins in a brief, loose turn.

changes.before: Sylvanas watched the wagon roll north past the last houses, the pale grain dust in the bed catching the last light.
changes.after: The wagon rolled north past the last houses, pale grain dust in the bed catching the last light.

changes.before: In another village, on another day, he would have been nothing but road talk and a creaking axle.
changes.after: On any other road, Sylvanas would have taken him for one more grain seller and kept riding.

changes.before: Still thin. Still loose. Enough to keep.
changes.after: The grain seller was still only a clue, but now two people had given Sylvanas the same one.

changes.before: The newer grain seller was still only a thread, but it was a thread from two mouths now.
